response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer with broad web, mobile, and some systems-level experience, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, and basic German), his C experience is brief and dated (a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and there is no Linux kernel, embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience anywhere in his history. His career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang web platforms, which is a poor match for embedded LAN-switch firmware development.
strengths: Nearly two decades of professional software development across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed, while native-level Japanese is a hard requirement
No Linux kernel analysis or modification experience, and C for UNIX proficiency is minimal and outdated
No experience with networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal), L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware for network devices

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol experience
Weak match on core C/UNIX and Linux kernel skills
